name: ON Semiconductor Corporation

profile.description: ON Semiconductor Corporation (onsemi) provides intelligent sensing and power solutions across automotive, industrial, AI data center, computing, and mobile end markets. The company operates through Power Solutions Group, Analog and Mixed-Signal Group, and Intelligent Sensing Group segments, specializing in silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride (GaN) power semiconductors, CMOS image sensors, and analog/mixed-signal ICs. Founded in 1992 and headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Onsemi has transformed from a legacy semiconductor company into a market leader in silicon carbide (SiC) power semiconductors for EVs and AI data centers under CEO Hassane El-Khoury, with AI data center revenue more than doubling year-over-year driven by broader adoption across the power tree with multiple chip vendors and leading hyperscalers [1]. The 200mm SiC production ramp represents a genuine technology leadership milestone that competitors are years behind replicating. AI Deployment (2/2): Onsemi's power management ICs are critical enabling technology for AI data centers — AI data center revenue exceeded $250M in 2025 and has more than doubled YoY, with products deployed across the full power tree from rack-level to chip-level power delivery for GPU-dense architectures [1]. The company's SiC technology reduces power losses by 50%+ versus silicon, directly enabling the energy efficiency that makes large-scale AI compute economically viable. Market Share (2/2): Onsemi holds a top-3 position in global SiC power semiconductors alongside Infineon and STMicroelectronics, with design wins at major automotive OEMs and hyperscale data center operators [1]. The company's integrated SiC supply chain from substrate to module provides structural cost advantages. Pipeline (1/2): The Treo Platform launch, 30+ GaN device samples planned for 2026, and first VGaN revenue expected in 2027 demonstrate a credible next-generation technology pipeline [1]. Management's strategic exit of $300M in non-core revenue in 2026 sharpens focus on high-value applications. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending is appropriate for a technology leader — the company invests heavily in SiC, GaN, and sensing technologies across 22,600 employees and multiple fabrication facilities.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Onsemi's mission of 'creating intelligent power and sensing technologies that solve the most challenging customer problems' connects to meaningful societal outcomes — EV electrification, energy efficiency, and AI infrastructure [2]. The mission is clear and identifies what the company does, and the focus on energy efficiency (SiC reduces power losses by 50%+) connects to climate impact. However, it reads as a corporate technology mission rather than an inspirational cause. Community Impact (1/2): Onsemi scored 85/100 from EcoVadis, ranking in the top 1% of semiconductor companies assessed, and has been included in the DJSI North America Sustainability Index and recognized as one of the World's Most Ethical Companies [3]. ESG rating upgraded from BBB to A, with strong practices in controversial sourcing and supply chain management. Employee Retention (1/2): The 22,600-employee workforce operates in the highly competitive semiconductor talent market where onsemi's SiC and AI data center leadership provides employer brand advantage [2]. However, the FabRite manufacturing consolidation program reduced fab capacity by 12% in 2025, likely creating workforce uncertainty. No specific Glassdoor data available for this review period. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO El-Khoury's strategic transformation — exiting $300M in non-core revenue, returning 100% of record $1.4B FCF through buybacks, and authorizing a new $6B repurchase program — demonstrates deep conviction in the company's long-term positioning and strong insider alignment [1].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Onsemi's financials show a company emerging from a semiconductor cyclical downturn with strong recovery indicators. Revenue of $6.06B TTM is growing 4.7% YoY, marking the first positive growth since the downturn began over three years ago, with management guiding Q1 2026 revenue of $1.44-1.54B suggesting continued acceleration [1][4]. D/E of 44.3% is healthy and conservative. ROE of 7.5% is recovering from the cyclical trough but still below the 15% target — management expects significant margin expansion as manufacturing utilization rises from the low-70s toward normalized 80%+ levels. Forward P/E of 28.4x reflects the stock's doubling from ~$61 to $122, pricing in recovery earnings. The standout metric is PEG of 0.4, indicating expected earnings growth substantially exceeds the valuation multiple, scoring at maximum as a 2x-weighted growth metric [4]. Profit margin of 9.5% is recovering from the 2.0% cyclical trough but remains below the 20% target. FCF of $1.28B demonstrates cash generation capability even during the downturn. Liquidity floor cleared easily: 11.4M avg vol x $121.62 = $1.39B daily [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Onsemi is a direct structural beneficiary of AI infrastructure expansion — its power management solutions are critical components in GPU-dense data center architectures, and AI data center revenue more than doubled year-over-year in 2025 [1]. SiC and GaN power devices enable efficient power delivery and conversion that AI compute demands — every AI training cluster and inference farm requires precision power management, and onsemi's products sit in the physical layer that software cannot replace. The company's automotive ADAS image sensors also benefit from AI-driven autonomous driving development. However, semiconductor commoditization risk from Chinese competitors expanding SiC capacity could pressure margins over time, preventing a perfect risk protection score [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Onsemi generates direct and growing AI revenue — AI data center power management exceeded $250M in 2025 and doubled YoY, with the company winning multiple chip vendor and hyperscaler design-ins [1]. The Treo Platform and GaN development specifically target AI infrastructure power requirements, and management has identified AI data centers as a core strategic growth pillar alongside automotive electrification.

infinite_game.summary: Onsemi demonstrates meaningful strategic long-term thinking through its technology roadmap, willingness to exit $300M in non-core revenue for focus, and counter-cyclical investment in SiC and GaN during a downturn. CEO El-Khoury's transformation from a legacy semiconductor company to a focused power and sensing platform reflects genuine strategic vision. However, the semiconductor industry's inherent cyclicality creates tension between long-term positioning and near-term performance pressure, and the current recovery phase is still early.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Onsemi's purpose — enabling electrification and AI through intelligent power and sensing technology — connects to genuine long-term societal impact [2]. SiC technology reduces power losses by 50%+ versus silicon, directly addressing climate change through semiconductor innovation. The company's products enable EV adoption, renewable energy systems, and energy-efficient AI infrastructure, representing technology that serves a future larger than any individual company. The DJSI inclusion and World's Most Ethical Companies recognition validate this purpose externally [3].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The 22,600-employee workforce operates through a transformation period that creates mixed trust signals [2]. The FabRite manufacturing consolidation reduced fab capacity by 12%, and the exit of $300M in non-core revenue likely displaced employees in affected business lines [1]. However, counter-cyclical investment in SiC and GaN during the downturn and the record FCF return through buybacks demonstrate financial commitment to the business that builds long-term confidence. No specific Glassdoor or employee satisfaction data was available.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Onsemi competes with Infineon, STMicroelectronics, and Wolfspeed in SiC, Texas Instruments in analog, and emerging Chinese competitors like BYD Semiconductor and StarPower [1]. Management's strategic response — exiting $300M in non-core revenue to focus on differentiated SiC/GaN capabilities and AI data center power — reflects a learn-and-improve mindset that acknowledges competitors' strengths rather than trying to compete everywhere. The decision to focus resources where onsemi has structural advantages is characteristic of infinite-game thinking.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The transformation under CEO El-Khoury from a broad-line legacy semiconductor company to a focused SiC/GaN power and sensing platform has been genuinely dramatic — the company exited non-core product lines, consolidated manufacturing, and repositioned around automotive electrification and AI data centers [1]. The addition of GaN and VGaN to the technology portfolio beyond SiC demonstrates continued strategic evolution. However, the concentration on automotive (50%+ of revenue) and the cyclical nature of semiconductors create vulnerability to sector-specific disruption.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Onsemi demonstrated genuine counter-cyclical courage — returning 100% of record $1.4B FCF to shareholders through buybacks and authorizing a new $6B repurchase program during a multi-year revenue decline shows financial conviction [1]. Investing in 30+ GaN samples and the Treo Platform while managing through cyclical weakness reflects willingness to sacrifice near-term margins for long-term technology positioning. The exit of $300M in revenue, accepting a 5% topline headwind for strategic focus, shows courage over convenience.

investment_thesis: Onsemi is emerging from a three-year semiconductor cyclical downturn with its strategic transformation largely complete and recovery accelerating. Revenue growth has turned positive at 4.7% YoY — the first positive quarter since the downturn began — with AI data center revenue more than doubling year-over-year and Q1 2026 guidance of $1.44-1.54B suggesting continued momentum [1]. The PEG ratio of 0.4 indicates expected earnings growth substantially exceeds the valuation multiple, and operating income increased 10% YoY, outpacing revenue growth by 2x as margins recover from cyclical lows [1]. The company's leadership position in SiC power semiconductors positions it at the intersection of two secular megatrends: automotive electrification and AI infrastructure power management. Record $1.4B FCF and a new $6B buyback authorization demonstrate financial strength, while the 200mm SiC production ramp provides a structural cost advantage competitors are years behind replicating. The Inspire Insight score of 71 with 20 positive ESG attributions reflects strong ALIGNED faith alignment, and the EcoVadis top 1% ranking confirms genuine ESG commitment [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation stretch, competitive risk, and cyclical uncertainty. The stock has doubled from ~$61 to $122 in three months, pushing the forward P/E from 15.1x to 28.4x — much of the recovery is now priced in, and trailing P/E of 89.4x reflects the depth of the earnings trough [4]. Profit margin at 9.5% is recovering but still well below historical peaks, and the timing and magnitude of full margin recovery through manufacturing utilization normalization remains uncertain. Chinese SiC competitors (BYD Semiconductor, StarPower) are rapidly expanding capacity and could undercut pricing in the automotive SiC market, compressing onsemi's margins even during recovery [1]. The strategic exit of $300M in non-core revenue creates a 5% topline headwind that must be offset by growth in AI and automotive focus areas. Automotive concentration at 50%+ of revenue creates vulnerability to EV adoption rate moderation from reduced consumer incentives, tariffs, or charging infrastructure delays. The semiconductor industry's inherent cyclicality means the recovery could stall if macro conditions deteriorate.
key_catalysts: AI data center power management revenue more than doubled YoY, with 200mm SiC production ramp and Treo Platform providing technology leadership — operating income grew 10% YoY, outpacing revenue by 2x as margins recover [1]
Manufacturing utilization recovery from low-70s toward normalized 80%+ would remove ~700bp of underutilization charges, potentially expanding gross margins by 500+ basis points through 2H 2026 [1]

PEG ratio of 0.4 signals significant expected earnings growth, supported by 30+ GaN device samples in 2026 and first VGaN revenue expected 2027, expanding onsemi's addressable AI and EV power market [1][4]
key_risks: Stock has doubled to $122 pushing forward P/E from 15.1x to 28.4x — recovery is increasingly priced in, and trailing P/E of 89.4x reflects fragile earnings that could disappoint if semiconductor cycle recovery stalls [4]
Chinese SiC competitors (BYD Semiconductor, StarPower) are rapidly expanding capacity and could undercut pricing in the automotive SiC market, compressing onsemi's margins during the recovery [1]

Automotive concentration at 50%+ of revenue creates vulnerability to EV adoption moderation from reduced incentives, tariffs, or infrastructure delays, while the $300M non-core revenue exit adds a 5% topline headwind [1]
sources: onsemi Q4/FY2025 Earnings Release and Q1 2026 Guidance — AI data center revenue doubled YoY, 10% operating income growth, $1.4B FCF, $6B buyback, 200mm SiC ramp, February 2026
onsemi CEO Hassane El-Khoury Strategic Vision, Technology Roadmap, Employee and Talent Market — Treo Platform, GaN pipeline, $300M non-core exit, 2025-2026
onsemi ESG Recognition — EcoVadis 85/100 top 1%, DJSI North America, World's Most Ethical Companies, MSCI ESG upgrade to A, 2019-2026
